summary.intent: Resolve all fixed/actionable PR review comments on a Rust codebase dealing with local LLM (llama.cpp) inference, including backend description fixes, code deduplication, debug logging, and backend concurrency improvements.
summary.outcome: Three of four actionable fixes were successfully implemented and committed: unknown GPU backend fallback in description/note functions, deduplication of meminfo helpers into a shared module, and a debug log for BackendAlreadyInitialized. The backend concurrency refactor using a shared static LlamaBackend was attempted and abandoned after a stash accident wiped the work; it was deemed a lower-priority theoretical race condition.

repo: llama-cpp-2 crate's LlamaBackend is an empty struct used only as proof-of-initialization; load_from_file takes &LlamaBackend but never reads from it
read_proc_meminfo and parse_meminfo_kb were duplicated verbatim between crates/providers/src/local_gguf/system_info.rs and crates/providers/src/local_llm/system_info.rs
GgufRuntimeDevice is defined in a pub(crate) module (runtime_devices), making it inaccessible for construction in gateway tests — requires making the module pub to write cross-crate tests
Ubuntu 22.04 (Jammy) ships glslang-tools but not glslc as a standalone package — CI must use glslang-tools
The project uses git worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/issue-122/

code.finding: gguf_backend_description and gguf_backend_note had no fallback for unknown GPU backends (e.g. ROCm/HIP), causing them to incorrectly report 'CPU inference' even when has_gpu()=true
Canonical location for shared read_proc_meminfo and parse_meminfo_kb helpers after deduplication; also contains the BackendAlreadyInitialized debug log
runtime_devices was pub(crate); had to be changed to pub to allow GgufRuntimeDevice construction in gateway integration tests

workflow: std::sync::OnceLock::get_or_try_init is unstable in stable Rust — must use OnceLock<Option<T>> with get_or_init and handle the error inside the closure
git stash pop during an investigation can silently drop all uncommitted changes if the stash was created at a clean state — always verify working tree before stashing
cargo check is fast enough to use as a compile gate before running tests; run cargo test --lib for unit tests and cargo test -p for crate-scoped tests

friction: git stash + pop wiped all in-progress uncommitted changes mid-session, requiring all edits to be redone from scratch
GgufRuntimeDevice was not constructible from the gateway crate due to pub(crate) module visibility, blocking cross-crate test writing and requiring a module visibility change
get_or_try_init on OnceLock is unstable, requiring a workaround with OnceLock<Option<T>> which is less ergonomic
Multiple failed Edit attempts on local_llm_setup.rs left the test section in a garbled state requiring a full rewrite of the affected block
The backend concurrency fix required deep inspection of the llama-cpp-2 crate internals (cargo registry source) to understand LlamaBackend's actual semantics before implementing
open_items: Backend concurrency fix (shared static LlamaBackend via OnceLock to prevent races between detect_runtime_support() and concurrent model loads) was intentionally deferred as a theoretical race with no reported crashes
The failing test test_register_saved_local_models_registers_custom_gguf_provider_from_saved_config in gateway was observed but not investigated — noted as possibly pre-existing from a merge
